name: Zeroheight Design System Documentation API
what_it_does: Design system documentation platform with REST API — enabling design teams to create living documentation for design systems by syncing Figma components, Storybook stories, and design tokens into a branded documentation site, with version control, multi-brand support, and team collaboration for design/development alignment.
use_cases: Syncing Figma design components to Zeroheight documentation via API
Managing design system pages and component documentation via Zeroheight API
Integrating Storybook stories with Zeroheight for unified design-code documentation
Querying design system documentation structure for governance reporting
Publishing design system updates and versioning documentation via Zeroheight API
not_for: Prototyping and wireframing (use Figma for design work — Zeroheight is for documentation)
Component development and testing (use Storybook directly for component development)
Version control for design files (use Abstract or Figma Branching for design version control)
best_when: A design team with an established Figma + Storybook design system needs a dedicated documentation site that stays in sync with both design and code — eliminating stale documentation.
avoid_when: You don't have a formal design system with Figma and Storybook, are a small team where Notion docs suffice, or need component testing rather than documentation.

auth.notes: API key from Zeroheight account settings. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. Webhook for documentation update events. Figma integration via Figma OAuth. Storybook integration via npm package. No OAuth2 for Zeroheight API. Design token sync via dedicated token management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team $149/month (up to 10 users); Business $299/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: London company. Founded 2017. Raised $10M Series A (2021). Backed by Index Ventures, Firstminute Capital. 3M+ designers and developers use Zeroheight. 2,000+ organizations. Figma and Storybook as primary data sources is key differentiator. Multi-brand design system support for enterprise. Design token version control. Used by ASOS, Adidas, BBC, Dell.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for styleguide, pages, and components. API key bearer auth. Good documentation for design system integration. Figma OAuth required for Figma sync. Storybook npm package for code integration. Webhook for documentation updates. Design token management via separate token API. Multi-brand support requires Business plan.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page reads fully idempotent. Component reads idempotent. Documentation sync operations are triggered updates — use sync job ID for status.
known_agent_gotchas: Figma OAuth setup required for design component sync — separate from Zeroheight API key
Storybook integration via npm package (@zeroheight/storybook-addon) — separate package install
Free tier limited to 1 styleguide — insufficient for multi-brand or multi-product design systems
Multi-brand support requires Business plan at $299/month
Documentation sync is asynchronous — Figma changes take time to propagate to Zeroheight

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. EU and US data residency. API key auth. UK company with GDPR-first design. Design system documentation may contain unreleased design patterns — restrict access. Figma OAuth scopes should be minimal (read-only for component sync). Storybook npm package in build pipeline — audit package security regularly.
